test_cases.title: Successful account upgrade from standard to premium
preconditions: User has a valid non-premium (standard) account
User is authenticated and logged in
A valid payment method or upgrade mechanism is available
steps: Log in with a standard (non-premium) user account
Navigate to the account upgrade section
Complete the upgrade process (e.g., confirm payment or subscription)
Observe the account status and available features
test_cases.expected_result: The user's account is marked as premium and additional features that were previously unavailable are now accessible.

test_cases.title: Premium-only features are inaccessible before upgrading
preconditions: User has a valid non-premium (standard) account
User is authenticated and logged in
steps: Log in with a standard (non-premium) user account
Attempt to access or use a feature designated as premium-only
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access to the premium feature and presents a prompt or message indicating that an account upgrade is required.

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated user cannot initiate account upgrade
preconditions: No user is logged in (guest or unauthenticated session)
steps: Without logging in, attempt to navigate directly to the account upgrade URL or endpoint
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies the upgrade request and redirects the user to login or returns an authentication error, without applying any upgrade.

test_cases.title: Already-premium user attempting to upgrade again
preconditions: User already has a premium account
User is authenticated and logged in
steps: Log in with an existing premium account
Navigate to the account upgrade section
Attempt to initiate the premium upgrade process
test_cases.expected_result: The system either prevents a duplicate upgrade or clearly communicates to the user that their account is already at premium status, without creating duplicate charges or account states.

test_cases.title: Upgrade process fails due to invalid or declined payment
preconditions: User has a valid non-premium (standard) account
User is authenticated and logged in
An invalid or declined payment method is available for use
steps: Log in with a standard (non-premium) user account
Navigate to the account upgrade section
Enter or select an invalid/declined payment method
Observe the account status and available features
test_cases.expected_result: The upgrade is not applied, the user's account remains at standard status, and the system displays an appropriate error message indicating the payment could not be processed.

assumptions: The upgrade process involves some form of payment or subscription confirmation, as 'premium' implies a paid tier.
There is a distinguishable set of features exclusive to premium accounts that are not available to standard users.
The system has at least two account tiers: standard (non-premium) and premium.
Users must be authenticated to perform an upgrade.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) what the additional premium features are, making it impossible to test specific feature unlocks precisely; (2) the upgrade mechanism (e.g., in-app purchase, subscription, one-time payment, or admin-granted); (3) whether a downgrade path exists; (4) how the system handles partial upgrades or network failures mid-transaction; (5) any trial or free premium period. Test cases for downgrade, cancellation, and feature reversion upon subscription expiry cannot be written without additional requirements.
